Nelson Piquet memorably described driving around Monaco as “like riding a bicycle around your living room”… which is fair. Despite that, it’s a challenge that nearly all drivers love, forcing them to put their skills on the line and rewarding millimetric accuracy. Overtaking on the tight streets is harder, however, with the 2003 Grand Prix witnessing a grand total of zero passing moves!</p></div></div></div></details><details name=":R2qn7rrrrncv8kq:" class="[&[open]_.f1rd-open]:block [&[open]_.f1rd-closed]:hidden border-t-thin [&:first-child]:border-t-0 border-surface-neutral-4" data-f1rd-a7s-click="qanda_interaction" data-f1rd-a7s-context="eyJhY3Rpb25UeXBlIjoib3BlbiIsImNsaWNrVGV4dCI6IldoeSBnbz8ifQ=="><summary class="cursor-pointer list-none [&::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden flex justify-between items-center min-h-[72px] py-rem-8"><p class="typography-module_body-m-compact-bold__Y0Jyw">Why go?</p><div class="f1rd-open hidden"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Up</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 14-6-6-6 6 1.4 1.4 4.6-4.6 4.6 4.6z"></path></svg></div><div class="f1rd-closed"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Dropdown</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 9.4-6 6-6-6L7.4 8l4.6 4.6L16.6 8z"></path></svg></div></summary><div class="mb-rem-16 pb-rem-8"><div class="px-px-24 max-w-para-lg"><div class="flex flex-col gap-rem-12 md:gap-rem-16 lg:gap-rem-24"><p class="typography-module_body-s-regular__ul7F5 typography-module_md_body-m-regular__d9VRg typography-module_lg_body-l-regular__dPa5z">A holiday on the French Riviera, plus the chance to watch Formula 1 cars pounding past places where Monegasques usually go to buy a pint of milk? It really is as fun as it sounds. On top of that, the chance to walk the track after a day’s hard lapping – or even enjoy your dinner on it at one of the <em>terrasse</em> bars – is not to be missed.</p></div></div></div></details><details name=":R2qn7rrrrncv8kq:" class="[&[open]_.f1rd-open]:block [&[open]_.f1rd-closed]:hidden border-t-thin [&:first-child]:border-t-0 border-surface-neutral-4" data-f1rd-a7s-click="qanda_interaction" data-f1rd-a7s-context="eyJhY3Rpb25UeXBlIjoib3BlbiIsImNsaWNrVGV4dCI6IldoZXJlIGlzIHRoZSBiZXN0IHBsYWNlIHRvIHdhdGNoPyJ9"><summary class="cursor-pointer list-none [&::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden flex justify-between items-center min-h-[72px] py-rem-8"><p class="typography-module_body-m-compact-bold__Y0Jyw">Where is the best place to watch?</p><div class="f1rd-open hidden"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Up</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 14-6-6-6 6 1.4 1.4 4.6-4.6 4.6 4.6z"></path></svg></div><div class="f1rd-closed"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" role="img"><title>Chevron Dropdown</title><path fill="currentColor" d="m18 9.4-6 6-6-6L7.4 8l4.6 4.6L16.6 8z"></path></svg></div></summary><div class="mb-rem-16 pb-rem-8"><div class="px-px-24 max-w-para-lg"><div class="flex flex-col gap-rem-12 md:gap-rem-16 lg:gap-rem-24"><p class="typography-module_body-s-regular__ul7F5 typography-module_md_body-m-regular__d9VRg typography-module_lg_body-l-regular__dPa5z">Assuming you can’t get into one of the overhanging apartment blocks around the circuit (or onto a boat) shoot for anything from Grandstands L to P, which are clustered around the Swimming Pool section of the track, and where drivers hurl their cars through the tight chicane at 200km/h. Or, for a more spectacular vista, try Grandstand B, overlooking the ritzy Casino Square.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="Container-module_container__0e4ac colors-module_bg_colour-surface-neutral-surface-neutral-3__u3lwa"><div class="Container-module_inner__UkLYJ"><div class="flex flex-col py-px-48 lg:py-px-64 gap-px-48 lg:gap-px-64"><h2 class="typography-module_display-2-xl-black__WHPIk Text-module_upper__pWFEw">MONACO</h2><ul class="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-px-8 md:gap-px-16 lg:gap-px-24 items-stretch" data-f1rd-a7s-context="eyJsb2NhdGlvbkluUGFnZSI6IlJhY2UgUHJvbW90aW9uYWwgQXJ0aWNsZXMifQ=="><li data-f1rd-a7s-click="promotional_content_click" data-f1rd-a7s-context="" class="DynamicArticleCard-module_card__GNumG DynamicArticleCard-module_overlay__CqnD6 DynamicArticleCard-module_constrained__m-x-r DynamicArticleCard-module_surface-neutral-1__Yj6F6 md:col-span-2" aria-describedby="title:S3:"><span class="DynamicArticleCard-module_image__N42Rw"><img src="https://media.formula1.com/image/upload/t_16by9South/c_fit,w_1584/q_auto/v1740000001/trackside-images/2026/F1_Grand_Prix_of_Monaco/2280325141.webp" alt="MONTE-CARLO, MONACO - JUNE 07: Esteban Ocon of France driving the (31) Haas F1 VF-26 Ferrari and Oliver Bearman of Great Britain driving the (87) Haas F1 VF-26 Ferrari chase the pack at the start during the F1 Grand Prix of Monaco at Circuit de Monaco on June 07, 2026 in Monte-Carlo, Monaco. The race was part of the calendar in the first year of the Formula 1 World Championship in 1950, and hasn’t been off it since 1955.\n\n### What’s the circuit like?\n\nIncredibly narrow and totally iconic. Nelson Piquet memorably described driving around Monaco as “like riding a bicycle around your living room”… which is fair. Despite that, it’s a challenge that nearly all drivers love, forcing them to put their skills on the line and rewarding millimetric accuracy. Overtaking on the tight streets is harder, however, with the 2003 Grand Prix witnessing a grand total of zero passing moves!\n\n### Why go?\n\nA holiday on the French Riviera, plus the chance to watch Formula 1 cars pounding past places where Monegasques usually go to buy a pint of milk? It really is as fun as it sounds. On top of that, the chance to walk the track after a day’s hard lapping – or even enjoy your dinner on it at one of the *terrasse* bars – is not to be missed.\n\n### Where is the best place to watch?\n\nAssuming you can’t get into one of the overhanging apartment blocks around the circuit (or onto a boat) shoot for anything from Grandstands L to P, which are clustered around the Swimming Pool section of the track, and where drivers hurl their cars through the tight chicane at 200km/h. The race was part of the calendar in the first year of the Formula 1 World Championship in 1950, and hasn’t been off it since 1955.\"},{\"heading\":\"What’s the circuit like?\",\"content\":\"Incredibly narrow and totally iconic. Nelson Piquet memorably described driving around Monaco as “like riding a bicycle around your living room”… which is fair. Despite that, it’s a challenge that nearly all drivers love, forcing them to put their skills on the line and rewarding millimetric accuracy. Overtaking on the tight streets is harder, however, with the 2003 Grand Prix witnessing a grand total of zero passing moves!\"},{\"heading\":\"Why go?\",\"content\":\"A holiday on the French Riviera, plus the chance to watch Formula 1 cars pounding past places where Monegasques usually go to buy a pint of milk? It really is as fun as it sounds. On top of that, the chance to walk the track after a day’s hard lapping – or even enjoy your dinner on it at one of the *terrasse* bars – is not to be missed.\"},{\"heading\":\"Where is the best place to watch?\",\"content\":\"Assuming you can’t get into one of the overhanging apartment blocks around the circuit (or onto a boat) shoot for anything from Grandstands L to P, which are clustered around the Swimming Pool section of the track, and where drivers hurl their cars through the tight chicane at 200km/h. Or, for a more spectacular vista, try Grandstand B, overlooking the ritzy Casino Square.\"}]},\"raceReview\":{\"raceResults\":[{\"driverTLA\":\"ANT\",\"driverFirstName\":\"Kimi\",\"driverLastName\":\"Antonelli\",\"teamName\":\"Mercedes\",\"positionNumber\":\"1\",\"raceTime\":\"2:23:31.243\",\"teamColourCode\":\"27f4d2\",\"gapToLeader\":\"0\",\"driverNameFormat\":\"FirstNameIsPrimary\"},{\"driverTLA\":\"HAM\",\"driverFirstName\":\"Lewis\",\"driverLastName\":\"Hamilton\",\"teamName\":\"Ferrari\",\"positionNumber\":\"2\",\"raceTime\":\"2:23:37.514\",\"teamColourCode\":\"e8002d\",\"gapToLeader\":\"6.271\",\"driverNameFormat\":\"FirstNameIsPrimary\"},{\"driverTLA\":\"GAS\",\"driverFirstName\":\"Pierre\",\"driverLastName\":\"Gasly\",\"teamName\":\"Alpine\",\"positionNumber\":\"3\",\"raceTime\":\"2:23:51.612\",\"teamColourCode\":\"00a1e8\",\"gapToLeader\":\"20.369\",\"driverNameFormat\":\"FirstNameIsPrimary\"}]},\"assemblyVideoListByTag\":{\"tags\":[{\"id\":\"F1:Race of Genoa.\"}]}]},{\"id\":\"1\",\"title\":\"When was its first Grand Prix?\",\"content\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"px-px-24 max-w-para-lg\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L86\",null,{\"text\":\"It was 1929 when racing engines first reverberated around the Principality, after cigarette manufacturer Antony Noghes decided to organise a race with his pals from the Automobile Club de Monaco. The race was part of the calendar in the first year of the Formula 1 World Championship in 1950, and hasn’t been off it since 1955.\"}]}]},{\"id\":\"2\",\"title\":\"What’s the circuit like?\",\"content\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"px-px-24 max-w-para-lg\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L86\",null,{\"text\":\"Incredibly narrow and totally iconic. Nelson Piquet memorably described driving around Monaco as “like riding a bicycle around your living room”… which is fair. Despite that, it’s a challenge that nearly all drivers love, forcing them to put their skills on the line and rewarding millimetric accuracy. Overtaking on the tight streets is harder, however, with the 2003 Grand Prix witnessing a grand total of zero passing moves!\"}]}]},{\"id\":\"3\",\"title\":\"Why go?\",\"content\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"px-px-24 max-w-para-lg\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L86\",null,{\"text\":\"A holiday on the French Riviera, plus the chance to watch Formula 1 cars pounding past places where Monegasques usually go to buy a pint of milk? It really is as fun as it sounds. On top of that, the chance to walk the track after a day’s hard lapping – or even enjoy your dinner on it at one of the *terrasse* bars – is not to be missed.\"}]}]},{\"id\":\"4\",\"title\":\"Where is the best place to watch?\",\"content\":[\"$\",\"div\",null,{\"className\":\"px-px-24 max-w-para-lg\",\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L86\",null,{\"text\":\"Assuming you can’t get into one of the overhanging apartment blocks around the circuit (or onto a boat) shoot for anything from Grandstands L to P, which are clustered around the Swimming Pool section of the track, and where drivers hurl their cars through the tight chicane at 200km/h.
